title: rubus × nudicaulis
intro: Rubus × nudicaulis, also known as the thimbleberry, is a hybrid plant species in the rose family, resulting from the cross between Rubus idaeus (red raspberry) and Rubus arcticus (arctic raspberry). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce pain, and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Rubus × nudicaulis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for various health conditions. Rubus × nudicaulis can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
